text: "Go down."... The contents of this chapter are of a more ancient date than those of the foregoing chapter: for the order of time is not always observed in the writings of the prophets.

"Galaad the head of Libanus."... By Galaad, a rich and fruitful country, is here signified the royal palace of the kings of the house of David: by Libanus, a high mountain abounding in cedar trees, the populous city of Jerusalem.

"Weep not for him that is dead."... He means the good king Josias, who by death was taken away, so as not to see the miseries of his country.—Him that goeth away, namely, Sellum, alias Joachaz, who was carried captive into Egypt.
"Write this man barren."... That is, childless: not that he had no children, but that his children should never sit on the throne of Juda.
